title: saxifraga marginata coriophylla
intro: Saxifraga marginata coriophylla, also known as the White Glacier Saxifrage, is a rare and endangered alpine plant species native to the Himalayas. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Saxifraga marginata coriophylla are said to include treating various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as its use in treating wounds and skin conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and used in teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
